user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: VanArsdel Ltd.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-12 08:53 EST
Subject: Follow-up: competitive positioning Review

There's been a change in our timeline for competitive positioning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-14 14:47 EST
Subject: Re: competitive positioning Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 17:57 EST
Subject: Re: competitive positioning Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-02-11
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Michelle (VanArsdel Ltd)

[00:01:30] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into competitive positioning.

[00:08:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] Michelle (VanArsdel Ltd): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:12:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:17:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Calendar Pre-Pack Automation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence
